description: Free from pretense or deceit; proceeding from genuine feelings. 'Thaemu' names the quality of heartfelt authenticity—expression that matches inner experience.
sound_symbolism: The word speaks from the heart. 'Tha' /θa/ begins with gentle breath, intention forming. The vowel 'e' opens to expression, feeling emerging. Then 'mu' grounds in truth, sincerity confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thaemu lira' (sincere words). Related to 'noemi' (authentic) but emphasizes the genuineness of expression rather than faithfulness to identity.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Sincere communication expresses actual feeling—pretense blocks connection while genuineness enables it.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sincere practice engages wholeheartedly—half-hearted effort cannot bring transformation.
